lesson.menu_desc: Talk about food and meals with how much, how many, some, any, a lot of -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Food & Healthy Eating -- quantities with how much, how many, some and any
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carolina Paludetto Rodrigues | Aula 19 -- Food & Healthy Eating

listenings.text: Hey Carolina, it's Mia! I'm so excited to make my fruit breakfast bowl with you this weekend. It is super easy and very fresh. First, you need some fruit. I always use a banana, an apple, and a lot of strawberries, because they are sweet and healthy. Then you add a little yogurt, but be careful: don't use too much sugar. I only add a little honey instead. After that, you put a few nuts on top for energy. The best part is that it's not heavy, so you feel light and ready for school. When I am hungry in the morning, this is my favorite snack. When I am thirsty, I drink a glass of water or orange juice with it. Try it and tell me how many strawberries you used! Talk soon, bye!

Good afternoon, students, and welcome to the school cooking club. Today I want to share three simple tips for healthy eating. First, eat a lot of fruit and vegetables every day, because they give you energy and keep you strong. Second, don't eat too much sugar. A little is fine, but too much is not good for you. Try fresh food instead of packaged snacks. Third, drink a lot of water, especially when you are thirsty after sports. Remember, you don't need a lot of money to eat well. A simple breakfast with some fruit and a few nuts is cheap, fresh and healthy. When you are hungry, choose a smart snack. Cook with us next week and learn how many colors you can put on one plate. See you then!

extra_audio.text: Mia is making a healthy fruit breakfast bowl. First, she takes some fresh fruit, like a banana and a lot of strawberries. Then, she adds a little yogurt and only a little honey, because too much sugar is not healthy. After that, she puts a few nuts on top for energy. Finally, she eats her snack and feels light and ready for school.
